Appeal Letter: My flight was cancelled and my travel insurance rejected the claim.
content:
Dear Grievance Redressal Officer,

Re: Claim Rejection for Flight Cancellation - Policy Number: [Policy Number]

I am writing to express my disappointment and seek a review of the claim rejection for my flight cancellation on [Date]. As per the policy terms, I was entitled to compensation for the cancellation of my flight due to [Reason for Cancellation].

However, the insurer has rejected my claim citing a pre-existing condition exclusion under clause 4.3. I would like to request that you review my case and provide a decision based on the policy terms and DGCA CAR Section 3 provisions.

I have attached the required documents, including the physician support letter and pre-authorization form, which were missing from the initial claim submission.

Please find the details of my claim below:

Policy Number: [Policy Number]

Claim Date: [Date]

Reason for Cancellation: [Reason for Cancellation]

I would appreciate it if you could expedite the review process and provide a decision at the earliest.

Thank you for your time and attention to this matter.

Sincerely,

[Your Name]

title: Complaint Email: My flight was cancelled and my travel insurance rejected the claim.
content:
Subject: Rejected Flight Cancellation Claim - Policy Number: [Policy Number]

Dear Customer Care,

I am writing to express my disappointment and seek assistance with my rejected flight cancellation claim. The claim was rejected due to a pre-existing condition exclusion under clause 4.3, but I believe that the policy terms and DGCA CAR Section 3 provisions support my claim.

I have attached the required documents, including the physician support letter and pre-authorization form, which were missing from the initial claim submission.

Please assist me in resolving this matter and provide a decision based on the policy terms and DGCA CAR Section 3 provisions.

Thank you for your time and attention to this matter.

Sincerely,

[Your Name]

title: Escalation Note: My flight was cancelled and my travel insurance rejected the claim.
content:
Internal Escalation Memo - Policy Number: [Policy Number]

To: Grievance Redressal Officer

Subject: Rejected Flight Cancellation Claim - Policy Number: [Policy Number]

The claim for flight cancellation on [Date] has been rejected by the insurer due to a pre-existing condition exclusion under clause 4.3. However, the policy terms and DGCA CAR Section 3 provisions support the claim.

The required documents, including the physician support letter and pre-authorization form, have been submitted to the insurer.

The matter has been escalated to the IRDAI IGMS portal and subsequently to the Insurance Ombudsman.

Please note that the GRO timeline is as follows:

* Initial claim submission: [Date]

* Rejection of claim: [Date]

* Submission of required documents: [Date]

* Escalation to IRDAI IGMS portal: [Date]

* Escalation to Insurance Ombudsman: [Date]

Please expedite the review process and provide a decision at the earliest.

Thank you for your time and attention to this matter.

Sincerely,

[Your Name]

title: Appeal Letter: My bank charged me twice for the same UPI transaction of Rs 5000 and refuses to ...
content:
Subject: Unauthorized Transaction of INR [amount] on [date] — Account/Card No. [xxxx] — Immediate Reversal Requested under RBI's Zero/Limited Liability Framework

Dear [Branch Manager/Nodal Grievance Officer],

I am writing to bring to your attention an unauthorized transaction of INR [amount] on [date] on my [account/credit card/debit card] ending [xxxx], which I did not authorize. I am reporting this within [X] hours/days of receiving the transaction alert.

I request that this be treated under the RBI's zero/limited liability framework, that the transaction be reversed (shadow reversal) within 10 working days as mandated, and that you confirm receipt of this complaint in writing with a complaint reference number.

I have already raised this in-app (reference [CRN]) without resolution within the stated TAT. I request immediate reversal of the debited amount.

Please find attached the necessary documents for your reference.

Sincerely,
[Your Name]

Evidence: Document 1: Bank's communication about the transaction, Document 2: Bank's blocking confirmation reference number

title: Appeal Letter: My bank charged me twice for the same UPI transaction and refuses a refund
content:
Subject: Unauthorized UPI Transaction - Request for Refund and Shadow Reversal

Dear [Bank's Nodal Grievance Officer],

I am writing to bring to your attention an unauthorized UPI transaction that was made from my account on [date]. The transaction was for [amount] and was made to [payee's UPI ID/name]. I have already raised this issue in-app (reference [CRN]) but have not received a resolution within the stated TAT.

I request that this be treated under the RBI's zero/limited liability framework, that the transaction be reversed (shadow reversal) within 10 working days as mandated, and that you confirm receipt of this complaint in writing with a complaint reference number.

Please find attached the necessary documents, including a screenshot of the transaction alert and a copy of the bank statement.

I would appreciate it if you could expedite this matter and provide a resolution at the earliest.

Thank you for your attention to this matter.

Sincerely,
[Your Name]

title: Appeal Letter: My bank charged me twice for the same transaction and won't refund it.
content:
Subject: Unauthorized Transaction Dispute - Request for Immediate Resolution

Dear [Bank's Nodal Grievance Officer],

I am writing to bring to your attention an unauthorized transaction dispute that I have been facing with my account. On [date], I noticed that I had been charged twice for the same transaction, which is unacceptable. I have attached the required documents to this complaint, including a statement excerpt showing the disputed line item and a screenshot of the transaction alert/SMS if amount mismatches.

I request that this be treated under the RBI's zero/limited liability framework, that the transaction be reversed (shadow reversal) within 10 working days as mandated, and that you confirm receipt of this complaint in writing with a complaint reference number.

I have blocked the card/freeze the account immediately via app, net banking, or the 24x7 helpline, and I have received a blocking confirmation reference number. I have also attached any merchant correspondence (cancellation confirmation, refund promise, non-delivery proof) to this complaint.

I would appreciate it if you could look into this matter urgently and provide a written explanation of the outcome, including the specific evidence relied upon if the dispute is rejected. I am available to provide any further information or evidence required to resolve this dispute.

Thank you for your prompt attention to this matter.

Sincerely,
[Account Holder's Name]

title:
Appeal Letter: Orbitel Communications
Network Service Incident Report — Repeated Call Drop & Da...
content:
Subject: Formal Appeal for Repeated Call Drops and Data Outages

Dear Orbitel Communications Customer Care Team,

I am writing to formally appeal the repeated call drops and data outages I have been experiencing since July 5, 2026. Despite the temporary load-balancing applied to the Velachery sector and the scheduled backhaul fiber replacement at Adyar micro-cell, the issue persists. I request a permanent resolution to this issue and compensation for the inconvenience caused.

I have attached the relevant documents, including the Network Service Incident Report and the evidence of repeated call drops and data outages. I request a response within 7 days, as per the TRAI regulations.

Sincerely,
Ananya Krishnan
Account Number: XXXXXX8823
Plan: Postpaid Unlimited 5G — 599

title: Appeal Letter: My health insurance claim was denied for a pre-existing condition exclusion even...
content:
Subject: Appeal for Claim Denial Due to Pre-Existing Condition Exclusion

Dear Aditya Birla Health Insurance Grievance Redressal Officer,

I am writing to appeal the claim denial for my health insurance policy due to pre-existing condition exclusion. According to the policy wordings, the waiting period for pre-existing diseases is 36 months from the date of inception of the first policy with the insurer. However, I disclosed my pre-existing condition at the time of policy purchase and have been continuously covered without any break.

I request that you review my case and provide a written response within the statutory response deadline of 30 days, as per the IRDAI regulations. I would appreciate it if you could provide a detailed explanation for the claim denial and any supporting documentation.

Sincerely,
[Your Name]

title: Complaint Email: My health insurance claim was denied for a pre-existing condition exclusion even...
content:
Subject: Claim Denial for Pre-Existing Condition Exclusion

Dear Aditya Birla Health Insurance Customer Support,

My claim for health insurance was denied due to pre-existing condition exclusion. I disclosed my condition at the time of policy purchase and have been continuously covered. I would like to request that you log a ticket and provide a reference number for this issue.

Thank you for your assistance.

Best regards,
[Your Name]

PRIOR ATTEMPT: Sent appeal letter to Aditya Birla Health Insurance Grievance Redressal Officer on [Date] with a response deadline of 30 days

IF NO RESPONSE BY DEADLINE: escalate via Internal appeal to the insurer's Grievance Redressal Officer (GRO) -> IRDAI Integrated Grievance Management System (IGMS) -> Insurance Ombudsman

NEXT ACTION: File a complaint with the IRDAI Integrated Grievance Management System (IGMS) within the statutory response deadline of 30 days

title: Appeal Letter: My flight was cancelled and the airline refused a refund
content:
To, The Duty Manager / Nodal Officer, [Airline Name]
Subject: Cancellation of flight [flight number], [date] — facilities and compensation claim
Dear Sir/Madam,

My flight [flight number] scheduled for [date/time] was cancelled with less than the required notice. I was not offered meals/refreshments as required under DGCA CAR Section 3 for a delay/cancellation of this duration. I request the applicable facility and, where a cancellation notice was inadequate, the compensation prescribed under CAR in addition to my refund or rebooking.

I have attached the relevant evidence, including my flight itinerary and ticket details, communication with the airline regarding the cancellation, and proof of payment for the flight. I expect a response within 7 working days.

Sincerely,
[Your Name]

title: Complaint Email: My flight was cancelled and the airline refused a refund
content:
Hi Airline Customer Support,

My flight was cancelled and I was not offered a refund. I would like to request a refund and compensation as per DGCA regulations. Please log a ticket/reference number for this issue.

Thank you,
[Your Name]

title: Escalation Note: My flight was cancelled and the airline refused a refund
content:
CASE: Passenger flight cancellation without adequate notice and facilities
PRIOR ATTEMPT: Appeal letter sent to Airline's customer relations/nodal officer on [date] with a response deadline of 7 working days
IF NO RESPONSE BY DEADLINE: escalate via Airline's customer relations / nodal officer -> DGCA AirSewa portal complaint -> Consumer forum (if compensation is refused)
NEXT ACTION: Submit a complaint through the AirSewa portal (airsewa.gov.in) and track the response.

CHRONOLOGY:
- Flight [flight number] scheduled for [date/time] was cancelled with less than the required notice.
- I was not offered meals/refreshments as required under DGCA CAR Section 3 for a delay/cancellation of this duration.
- I requested a refund and compensation as per DGCA regulations, but was refused.

RELIEF SOUGHT: Refund and compensation as per DGCA regulations, and a written explanation of the airline's actions.

title: Appeal Letter: My flight was cancelled and the airline refused a refund

Subject: Cancellation of flight [flight number], [date] — facilities and compensation claim
Dear Sir/Madam,
My flight [flight number] scheduled for [date/time] was cancelled with less than the required notice. I was not offered meals/refreshments and hotel accommodation as required under DGCA CAR Section 3 for a delay/cancellation of this duration. I request the applicable facility and, where a cancellation notice was inadequate, the compensation prescribed under CAR in addition to my refund or rebooking. I expect a response within 48 hours.
Sincerely,
[Your Name]

content:

PRIOR ATTEMPT: Appeal letter/complaint email will be sent to the Airline's customer relations / nodal officer within 48 hours, with a response deadline of 48 hours.
IF NO RESPONSE BY DEADLINE: escalate via Airline's customer relations / nodal officer -> DGCA AirSewa portal complaint -> Consumer forum (if compensation is refused).
NEXT ACTION: Submit a complaint to the DGCA AirSewa portal within 30 days of the initial complaint, providing all relevant evidence and documentation.

content:

Contact Number: [Your Contact Number]
RESPONDENT: [Airline Name]
REFERENCE/ACCOUNT NUMBER: [Flight Number]
NATURE OF COMPLAINT: Entitlement to compensation and refund under DGCA CAR Section 3 due to inadequate notice and facilities.
CHRONOLOGY:
- Flight [flight number] scheduled for [date/time] was cancelled with less than the required notice.
- No meals/refreshments and hotel accommodation were offered as required under DGCA CAR Section 3 for a delay/cancellation of this duration.
- Request for compensation and refund was made to the Airline's customer relations / nodal officer.
RELIEF SOUGHT: Compensation and refund under DGCA CAR Section 3.

title: Appeal Letter: My bank HDFC charged me twice for the same transaction and won't refund it.
content:
To, The Branch Manager / Nodal Grievance Officer, HDFC Bank, [Branch/Account details]

Subject: Unauthorized transaction of INR 12,500 on [date] — Account/Card No. [xxxx] — Immediate reversal requested under RBI's zero/limited liability framework

Dear Sir/Madam,

I am writing to bring to your attention an unauthorized transaction of INR 12,500 on [date] on my [account/credit card/debit card] ending [xxxx], which I did not authorize. I am reporting this within [X] hours/days of receiving the transaction alert. I request that this be treated under the RBI's zero/limited liability framework, that the transaction be reversed (shadow reversal) within 10 working days as mandated, and that you confirm receipt of this complaint in writing with a complaint reference number.

I have taken immediate action to block the card/freeze the account via app, net banking, or the 24x7 helpline, and I have obtained a blocking confirmation reference number. I have also filed a cyber crime complaint at cybercrime.gov.in or dial 1930 (national cyber fraud helpline) for the reference number.

I expect a response from your team within 10 working days, and I request that you provide a detailed explanation of the actions taken to resolve this issue. If the issue remains unresolved, I will escalate this matter to the RBI Integrated Ombudsman Scheme.

Please acknowledge receipt of this complaint and provide a complaint reference number.

Sincerely,
[Your Name]

title: Complaint Email: My bank HDFC charged me twice for the same transaction and won't refund it.
content:
Hi HDFC Bank Customer Support,

I'm writing to report an unauthorized transaction of INR 12,500 on [date] on my [account/credit card/debit card] ending [xxxx]. I'd like you to reverse the transaction immediately under RBI's zero/limited liability framework and provide a complaint reference number. Please log a ticket/reference number for this issue.

Thanks,
[Your Name]

content:

IF NO RESPONSE BY DEADLINE: escalate via Bank's internal grievance redressal / nodal officer — contact listed on HDFC Bank's website 'Customer Grievance' page and on your passbook/statement -> RBI Integrated Ombudsman Scheme: file online at cms.rbi.org.in, or call the RBI complaint toll-free number 14448

NEXT ACTION: File a complaint with the RBI Integrated Ombudsman Scheme online at cms.rbi.org.in or call the RBI complaint toll-free number 14448

title: Appeal Letter: My bank ICICI charged me twice for the same transaction and won't refund it.
content:
To, The Branch Manager / Nodal Grievance Officer, ICICI Bank, [Branch/Account details]

Subject: Unauthorized transaction of INR [amount] on [date] — Account/Card No. [xxxx] — Immediate reversal requested under RBI's zero/limited liability framework

Dear Sir/Madam,

I am writing to bring to your attention an unauthorized transaction of INR [amount] on [date] on my [account/credit card/debit card] ending [xxxx], which I did not authorize. I am reporting this within [X] hours/days of receiving the transaction alert. I request that this be treated under the RBI's zero/limited liability framework, that the transaction be reversed (shadow reversal) within 10 working days as mandated, and that you confirm receipt of this complaint in writing with a complaint reference number.

I have taken immediate steps to block the card/freeze the account via app, net banking, or the 24x7 helpline, and I have obtained a blocking confirmation reference number. I have also filed a cyber crime complaint at cybercrime.gov.in or dial 1930 (national cyber fraud helpline) — this reference number strengthens the bank complaint and is often required for reversal above a threshold.

I expect a written response from you within 30 days, acknowledging receipt of this complaint and confirming the steps you will take to resolve the issue. I request that you provide a written explanation of the outcome, including the specific evidence relied upon if the dispute is rejected.

Sincerely,
[Your Name]
